title: Northern Norway is ready to launch. EU Space Regulation — and its new Arctic policy – is not.
summary: Northern Norway is prepared for space launches, but the EU's updated Arctic policy, expected this autumn, may not align with these plans. The European Commission's new policy will reportedly emphasize different priorities compared to the 2021 version.

title: Shenzhou-23 crew arrives at Tiangong as China maps path to 2030 lunar landing
summary: Three Chinese astronauts arrived at the Tiangong space station, with one expected to stay in orbit for a year. China also outlined plans for a 2030 lunar landing.

title: NASA to add missions to SpaceX commercial crew contract
summary: NASA intends to extend SpaceX's commercial crew contract by adding more missions. This move aims to mitigate risks if Boeing's spacecraft fails certification for ISS missions.

title: Blue Origin completes investigation into New Glenn launch failure
summary: Blue Origin has concluded the investigation into the New Glenn rocket's third flight failure, allowing the vehicle to resume launches. The investigation's completion paves the way for future missions.

title: SpaceX launches first Starship V3
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the first Starship V3 on May 22, achieving most of the test objectives during the suborbital flight. This marks a significant milestone in the development of SpaceX's next-generation spacecraft.

title: Space Force awards Viasat, SES $437 million for military satellite network
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded Viasat and SES a $437 million contract to build four small geostationary satellites for the Protected Tactical Satcom-Global program. This initiative aims to enhance military satellite communications.

title: NASA unveils sweeping reorganization
summary: NASA announced a major reorganization on May 22, combining mission directorates and reshuffling personnel. This marks one of the agency's largest restructurings in recent years.

title: Leveraging AUKUS and southern geography: building Australia’s dual-use space infrastructure for strategic resilience
summary: Australia is developing dual-use space infrastructure, including the Deep Space Advanced Radar Capability (DARC) Site 1 in Western Australia, to enhance strategic resilience in collaboration with AUKUS partners. The site is already providing early tracking data and aims for full operational capability by 2027.

title: SpaceX IPO filing casts Starlink Mobile as future wireless challenger
summary: SpaceX's IPO filing highlights Starlink Mobile as a potential competitor to terrestrial wireless networks, aiming to provide direct-to-smartphone services comparable to urban mobile networks.

title: Rocket Lab launches ninth Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its ninth radar imaging satellite for Synspective. The company also plans to sell up to $3 billion in stock to fund future initiatives.

title: DARPA readies robotic deep-space repair satellite for 2026 launch
summary: DARPA is preparing a robotic satellite for a 2026 launch to repair geosynchronous satellites, extending their operational lifespan. The mission aims to test the technology as early as this summer.

title: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' wastes a potentially brilliant era of 'Star Wars'
summary: The latest 'Star Wars' film, 'The Mandalorian and Grogu,' is criticized for squandering a promising era in the franchise by focusing on less engaging aspects of the galaxy. The movie fails to fully capitalize on its potential.

title: What is the blue-white star next to the moon tonight?
summary: On May 26, the waxing moon will be joined by the bright blue-white star Spica in the southern sky. This celestial event offers a chance to observe the star near the moon.

title: Nothing beats a beautiful sunset (from space) | Space photo of the day for May 26, 2026
summary: A space photo showcasing a beautiful sunset viewed from space is featured as the 'Space photo of the day' for May 26, 2026. The image highlights the stunning visuals of Earth's atmosphere from an orbital perspective.

title: Scientists just found a supercharged supernova — powered up by a magnetic star corpse
summary: NASA's Fermi Gamma-ray spacecraft observed a supercharged supernova powered by a magnetar, a highly magnetic dead star. This discovery highlights the extreme energy release from such cosmic events.

title: Scientists want to send a roly-poly robot filled with 'dandelion drones' to investigate hidden tunnels on Mars
summary: Scientists propose sending a roly-poly robot equipped with 'dandelion drones' to explore hidden tunnels on Mars. The design draws inspiration from biomimicry, a growing trend in Mars robotics.

title: The original (and best) 'Transformers' movie is rolling back out into theaters for its 40th anniversary
summary: The original 'Transformers' movie is re-releasing in theaters for its 40th anniversary. Fans can relive the animated cult classic featuring Autobots and Decepticons.

title: 15 sci-fi books to read before you die: Put these science fiction gems on your bucket list
summary: A list of 15 recommended science fiction books is provided to help readers prioritize their reading bucket list. The article aims to highlight quality sci-fi books amid the overwhelming number of new releases.

title: Grab the Estes Blue Origin model rocket ahead of Memorial Day with it's lowest price since January!
summary: The Estes Blue Origin New Shepard model rocket is on sale for $29.99 on Amazon, its lowest price since January. The discount makes it cheaper than the official Estes webstore.

title: Lunar Strike offers a pessimistic, and worryingly realistic look at the future of space travel
summary: The article titled 'Lunar Strike' presents a bleak and realistic perspective on the challenges and risks associated with future space travel. It highlights concerns about the feasibility and safety of lunar missions.

title: Call for Creatives: NASA Seeks Help Illuminating Mission Storytelling
summary: NASA is seeking creative partners to help tell the stories of its missions, including Artemis Moon missions and nuclear propulsion, through various mediums like film, music, and poetry. The agency issued a call for proposals on May 21 to collaborate with storytellers.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Roscosmos Spacewalk Outside Space Station
summary: NASA will broadcast a live spacewalk by two Roscosmos cosmonauts outside the International Space Station on May 27, starting at 10:15 a.m. EDT and lasting about five hours. Coverage will be available on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and NASA’s YouTube channel from 9:45 a.m.

title: Webb Studies Star Clusters
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope observed nearly 9,000 star clusters in four nearby galaxies, including Messier 51. The study reveals that more massive star clusters are more common.

title: Keeping NASA Flying: Ground Crews Ensure Aircraft Readiness
summary: NASA relies on ground crews at Armstrong Flight Research Center to maintain aircraft for research and science missions. The center added two new aircraft to its fleet this year.

title: NASA to Compete Contract for Jet Propulsion Laboratory Management
summary: NASA plans to compete the next contract for managing the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), currently operated by Caltech since its inception. The move aims to ensure accountability and maximize value for U.S. taxpayers.

title: NASA Announces Realignment to Accelerate Mission Delivery
summary: NASA announced an agencywide realignment to accelerate mission delivery and align with national space policy goals. The changes aim to improve efficiency and focus on high-priority objectives.

title: New Material Could Help NASA Melt Moon Rocks, Harness Lunar Resources
summary: A new material developed at NASA's Glenn Research Center could enable astronauts to melt Moon rocks and extract valuable resources like metals and oxygen. This innovation supports NASA's goal of utilizing lunar resources for future missions.

title: Hubble Captures Galaxy Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy cluster with galaxies of various shapes and sizes. Foreground stars are distinguished by diffraction spikes.

title: NASA to Showcase Mission to Boost Swift Spacecraft’s Orbit
summary: NASA will showcase a mission to boost the orbit of its Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory using Northrop Grumman’s Pegasus XL rocket and a Katalyst robotic spacecraft. The LINK servicing spacecraft is set to launch in June to rendezvous with the observatory.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo satellite launches dominating the manifest.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 aboard the International Space Station has been busy with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance activities through April. The crew's activities have been highlighted in a recent report.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to the Chinese Tiangong space station, which has been continuously occupied since June 5, 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, at 5:30:21 pm CDT from SpaceX's facilities. The mission demonstrated significant improvements and impressed observers during its inaugural flight.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled worldwide in the coming week, including SpaceX's Starship Flight 12, Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Arianespace's Vega C. The missions highlight ongoing activity in the commercial and international space sectors.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Arianespace's Vega C rocket to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of Starship, highlighting its ongoing improvements. It is presented as a key development in SpaceX's future launch capabilities.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is actively planning Artemis III, a significant crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key tests for the Human Landing System (HLS).

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. This follows multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company surpassed all its 2025 milestones during this period.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified topic related to the space industry. No specific details about the news content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates key milestones and contributions to space exploration and science.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Researchers in ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section, including many young minds, test materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for the agency's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions and education.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute deployment test, likely related to a Mars or planetary landing, is being conducted. The 'bake-out' process may refer to thermal testing of the parachute system.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter diameter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life on Mars.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, confirming its readiness for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: The US Needs a New Launch Site, Air Force Secretary Says
summary: The US Air Force Secretary has stated that current military launch sites are nearing full capacity, necessitating the development of a new launch site. This highlights the growing demand for space launch capabilities.

title: ESA Chief Calls on Europe to Double Funds for Space
summary: ESA's chief urges Europe to double its funding for space initiatives, stating that current investments are insufficient despite past growth in public funds.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's recent stakeholder survey identified improved lunar technology as the top priority for future space tech investments. The agency sought community input to address key technical challenges.

title: ESA Approves Two Scout Science Missions
summary: ESA has approved two Scout science missions aimed at conducting groundbreaking research with cost-effective solutions. The missions demonstrate Europe's commitment to affordable scientific exploration.

title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX disclosed $18.67 billion in revenue for 2025 in SEC filings ahead of its upcoming IPO. The company is expected to go public next month.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers warn that the Golden Dome missile defense system could escalate the nuclear arms race. The system is intended to protect the U.S. but may provoke adversaries.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests that NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve Chinese researchers, potentially posing a security risk. The findings highlight concerns over collaborations that could compromise national security.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving decision-making processes in design reviews. This shift aims to enhance efficiency and reduce complexity in engineering production.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: The Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF) forecasts a potential shortage in launch supply, with annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000, requiring up to 7,000 launches per year depending on the scenario.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, has launched a new satellite bus business line to sell satellite buses targeting low-cost, high-volume, and high-power missions across communications, Earth observation, and national security programs.

title: ST Engineering iDirect’s New CEO Kuppanna Talks Ground Tech’s Cloud-Native, Interoperable Future
summary: ST Engineering iDirect appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO in late March to lead the company amid a changing ground technology market. Kuppanna emphasized the future of cloud-native and interoperable solutions in ground tech.

title: UK Inaugurates New Space Domain Awareness Software Borealis
summary: The UK has launched a new space domain awareness software called Borealis, which is now operational six months ahead of schedule. The software enhances the tracking of satellites and space debris for the U.K. government.

title: Rocket Lab to Build First GEO Satellites for Space Force Space Domain Awareness Mission
summary: Rocket Lab has secured its first contract to build two Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites for the U.S. Space Force's space domain awareness mission, valued at $90 million.

title: Viasat Completes D2D Demo in Uzbekistan
summary: Viasat has successfully completed a direct-to-device (D2D) demonstration in Uzbekistan, showcasing messaging capabilities on Android smartphones via its L-band satellite technology.

title: LatConnect 60 Looks to Raise Up to $20M in Growth Round to Fund SWIR Constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 is seeking to raise between $5 million and $20 million in a growth funding round to support its short-wave infrared (SWIR) satellite constellation. The company aims to accelerate its satellite development efforts through this investment.

title: SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge
summary: The Space Force’s Space Systems Command (SSC) and SpaceWERX are launching an In-Domain Orbital Logistics Challenge to advance commercial technologies for satellite sustainment. The initiative aims to identify and foster innovations in orbital logistics.

title: Planet is Building an Agentic Geospatial AI Query
summary: Planet is developing an AI-powered geospatial query system that allows users to ask natural language questions about geographic data, such as identifying new construction sites in Florida. This agentic AI aims to enhance accessibility and usability of geospatial information.

title: Amadeus IT Group to Use Spire’s Aircraft Tracking Data for Airport Management Tool
summary: Amadeus IT Group will integrate Spire Global's aircraft tracking data into its cloud platform for airport management. This collaboration aims to enhance airport operations using Spire's aviation intelligence.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Filing Gives First Look Into Company’s Financials
summary: SpaceX released initial financial documents ahead of a planned IPO, offering the public the first detailed look at its financials. The filing provides insights into the company's financial health and future plans.

title: China’s Home Field Advantage at WRC-27 Worries US Operators
summary: China's hosting of the 2027 World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC-27) raises concerns among U.S. space operators due to its potential influence over critical satellite agenda items. The event is expected to heavily focus on the growing space sector.
